etymology.description: 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") refers to flame or heat, and 鍋(鍋) (guō, "pot") is a vessel used for cooking.
etymology.interpretation: 火(huǒ, "fire") is linked to heating or cooking, representing the method of preparing the food. 鍋(guō, "pot") denotes the container in which the ingredients are prepared. Together, 火鍋 refers to a pot heated by fire or flame, which is the defining trait of the hot pot dining experience where food is cooked directly at the table in a simmering pot.

examples.chinese: 我们一起去吃火锅(我們一起去吃火鍋)。
examples.english: Let's go to eat hot pot together.

examples.chinese: 天气冷的时候(天氣冷的時候)，吃火锅最舒服(吃火鍋最舒服)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānqì lěng de shíhòu, chī huǒguō zuì shūfú.
examples.english: When the weather is cold, eating hot pot is the most comfortable.

examples.chinese: 昨晚我在家里涮火锅(昨晚我在家裡涮火鍋)。
examples.pinyin: Zuó wǎn wǒ zài jiā lǐ shuàn huǒguō.
examples.english: Last night I made hot pot at home.
